author: @srossnz
text:
Hi Robert, when you see headlines like this: 

"Fracking gets U.K. approval in effort to go 'all out for shale'. Moratorium on controversial fracking process ends as Britain seeks energy self-sufficiency"

What do you think or feel?  We've never needed a new energy source more than now.  They are also ramping up fracking here in "Clean, Green New Zealand" what a joke.  I wish you guys all the best and hope one day technologies such as graphene super capacitors put an end to this raping of the environment which we must live in.  Something must change very soon, or we're doomed at hand of these greedy bastards :(

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Sean Ross I think we are already at beginning of a paradigm shift mate - more and more people are becoming aware of the need for it and i think it will come sooner rather than later

author: @ScottSextonandDavidLewis
text:
Robert let me know I posted without permission for comments, and since I can't seem to change the prior comments permissions, I'm reposting here with public enabled:

Indeed you have worked hard Rob, and the results and rewards are still coming in. You deserve them. Your last post sets the stage so well. It was carefully organized and that seems to come natural to you. I tip my hat to you again and again.
Still hoping you can see the movie Tomorrowland. I think you might find little bits of it quite fun even as the majority finds it boring, too forward thinking, oddly intellectual, and promoting women in science and equality in general. Blue Peter and Sunvault and Tomorrowland and RMS. In my mind, these things have real and hidden connections.

Cheers mate, before you say it.
p.s. Wondering if you have your Raman Spectrograph for graphene quality checking yet?

author: @ScottSextonandDavidLewis
text:
Oops. Forgot to share this on Google+ the first time:
p.s. I finally etched some aluminum and am started on making solid electrolyte for my graphene supercap experiment. All thanks to your motivation and organization and more. Now I have to travel for a week and the experiment will all get put on hold---bummer. But I'm making tiny strides. Cheers again to you mate.

author: @ScottSextonandDavidLewis
text: p.s. I *finally* etched some aluminum and am started on making solid electrolyte. All thanks to your motivation and organization and more. Now I have to travel for a week and the experiment will all get put on hold---bummer. But I'm making tiny strides. Cheers again to you mate.

author: @ScottSextonandDavidLewis
text: Forgot to ask:  Did you get your Raman Spectrometer?  I still don't know exactly how it verifies the quality of a batch of graphene, but I know you do. So I hope you get that 'little' piece of important equipment via some Sunvault stock or some other reward for your work.

author: @ScottSextonandDavidLewis
text:
Indeed you have worked hard Rob, and the results and rewards are still coming in. You deserve them. Your last post sets the stage so well. It was carefully organized and that seems to come natural to you. I tip my hat to you again and again.
Still hoping you can see the movie Tomorrowland. I think you might find little bits of it quite fun even as the majority finds it boring, too forward thinking, oddly intellectual, and promoting women in science and equality in general. Blue Peter and Sunvault and Tomorrowland and RMS. In my mind, these things have real and hidden connections.

Cheers mate, before you say it.
